questions: Concern 1: Should the migration utility (migrateEffectsToResources) be built now, or is the deprecation warning + ordered registration sufficient for this pass? Building the migration adds scope but solves the problem permanently.

Concern 4: Where is EffectCreatorPanel rendered — is the timeline output config (fps, resolution) readily available at that call site, or would threading it require prop drilling through multiple layers?

Is there a preference for where shared param validation logic lives — should getFallbackValue be extracted from ParameterControls into a shared utility, or is duplicating the small switch statement in validateParams.ts acceptable?

The effect-store.ts draft path: if grep confirms saveDraftEffect/promoteDraftToTimeline have no active callers, should we remove the draft source from useEffectRegistry entirely (still keeping the file for backwards compat), or keep loading drafts?

success_criteria.criterion: All 4 existing test files pass without modification: DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx, useEffectResources.test.tsx, VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx, registration.test.ts
DynamicEffectRegistry exposes subscribe/getVersion and notifies listeners on register/registerAsync
useEffectRegistry uses useSyncExternalStore (or equivalent) so components re-render when async effect registration completes
Resource effects registered after legacy DB effects so resources win on name collision
EffectErrorBoundary wraps effect rendering in wrapWithClipEffects and EffectLayerSequence — runtime throws show error overlay instead of crashing composition
validateAndFillParams fills missing params with schema defaults and coerces types before passing to effect components
parameterSchema stored in DynamicEffectRegistry alongside effect components and retrievable via getSchema()
Existing clips referencing effects by slug (legacy) or custom:{resourceId} continue to resolve correctly
Built-in effects always take priority over dynamic effects with the same name
Legacy effects table path has deprecation warning when effects are loaded
EffectCreatorPanel preview uses actual timeline FPS and aspect ratio when available, falling back to current defaults
New test coverage for reactive registry (subscribe/notify), EffectErrorBoundary (catch + render fallback), and validateAndFillParams (defaults, coercion, clamping)
Dual normalization (resolveEffectName + normalizeName) consolidated to single path
No measurable render performance regression from ErrorBoundary wrapper in compositions
Each new/modified file stays under ~300 lines
Manual smoke test: create AI-generated effect in EffectCreatorPanel, apply to clip, verify preview and timeline both render correctly
Manual smoke test: effect with intentional runtime error shows error overlay instead of crashing timeline

assumptions: The legacy effects table still has data for some users — we deprecate with warnings but do not remove the read path.
Draft effects (localStorage) are still loaded but the save/promote paths are likely vestigial — we'll verify with grep and mark deprecated if confirmed, but won't remove loading.
getFallbackValue logic is small enough (~15 lines) to duplicate in validateParams.ts rather than extracting a shared module, avoiding a refactor of ParameterControls.
ErrorBoundary class component is acceptable (React error boundaries require class components) — no need for a library dependency.
The EffectCreatorPanel is rendered in a context where timeline output config (fps, resolution) can be threaded through without excessive prop drilling.
useSyncExternalStore is available (React 18+) in this codebase.
The migration utility (migrateEffectsToResources) is deferred to a follow-up — this pass establishes ordering and deprecation warnings.
